old_claim.note: Mathematics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Although the tablet includes written distance statements, its catalogue does not establish mathematical calculation as a method required to produce the mapped cosmography.

source_shape.source_locator: Object 1882,0714.509, Description: written notes place six or seven beru between outer regions, while the catalogue highlights inconsistencies between map notes and reverse text and does not describe a calculated scale, coordinates, or mathematical model.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Written distance labels are present, but the object record does not document a mathematical cartographic method.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Numerals appearing in an inscription support writing and annotation; they do not justify a broad mathematics prerequisite without evidence of calculation governing the map's construction.

ontology_before: The graph treated mathematics as a generic enabler for all cartography rather than testing the cited artifact's method.
ontology_after: The node retains the map's written distance annotations without turning them into an unsupported mathematical-production dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-babylonian-world-map-boundary: mathematics is not a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A reliable analysis demonstrates that mathematical calculation or scale governed the construction of the Babylonian World Map.
short_reason: Distance annotations alone do not evidence mathematical map construction.
